title: Anime list — running 2020→2025 watchlist
html:
TL;DR

  • Living anime watchlist started Aug 2020, last edited Sep 2025 — 5 years of accumulated taste data. Includes all the genre-defining canon (One Piece absent, Berserk + movies, Cowboy Bebop, Vinland Saga, Death Note, Hunter x Hunter) plus contemporary picks (Chainsaw Man, Frieren-adjacent, Sakamoto Days, Dan Da Dan).
  • Heavy bias toward dark-fantasy / battle-shonen with intellectual undertones: Monster, Death Note, Vinland Saga, Berserk, Mob Psycho, Erased.
  • The note's continued maintenance through Sep 2025 (mid-Austin chapter) indicates anime is a stable identity thread, not a passing teen interest.
  • Includes the curiously-titled "Good Night Wrld" and "Wrld Trigger" — Yahir's chronic "Wrld" spelling is here too, before 2024 07 12 wrld side quests formalized it.
  • Strong overlap with the dystopian/lone-protagonist archetype that shows up in his self-talk (e.g. 2026 02 26 motherfucker falls backwards — "falls backwards into the void with a smile on his face").

Key quotes

"Berserk Movies" / "Vinland Saga 2 Seasons" / "Monster 1 Season"

"Good Night Wrld 1 Season" / "Wrld Trigger"

(Note has no prose, only a list. The taste-pattern is the artifact.)

Why it matters in the graph

  • Stable taste-profile signal across 5 years. Useful for personality / aesthetic prediction in any future synthesis.
  • The lone-warrior-in-a-broken-world genre matches Yahir's self-positioning in his hardest journals (e.g. 2025 03 15 than usual recently — "I'm just on a treadmill").
  • Cross-reference with the "Wrld" stylized spelling that became 2024 07 12 wrld side quests and shows up in T-shirt ideas in 2024 07 04 questions.

Open questions

  • Did he actually finish Berserk? (Most people don't.)
  • Has he tracked which of these landed deepest? Worth asking.
  • Why is One Piece notably absent? Is it deliberate?

Wikilinks

grants pass teen era · 2024 07 12 wrld side quests
